message:
test: transaction-wrap oracle seed rows + add aggregated_hot_path to benchmark-matrix fixture

Four small test-surface fixes landed together because they all
unblock the same `cargo test --all-targets` run that dep-upgrade
validation caught: three are pure test hygiene, the fourth absorbs
a struct-field drift in BenchmarkSummary.

crates/fsqlite-core/tests/conformance_oracle_ext.rs
- test_conformance_fk_cascade_3_plus_levels_s76c: wrap the 8 seed
  INSERT statements in an explicit `BEGIN` / `COMMIT`. Each seed
  insert was running in implicit autocommit, paying the full
  txn-commit cost per row — on slower hosts that pushed the test
  over its time budget. Wrapping in one txn drops insert time to
  a single commit.
- Removes a now-obsolete comment ("Seed with single-row inserts
  to avoid multi-row INSERT issues") that referred to a limitation
  fixed months ago; the inserts stay one-per-row because the test
  asserts individual row shapes.

crates/fsqlite-core/tests/conformance_oracle_s74b.rs
- test_conformance_insert_returning_s74ar: collapse the
  `for s in &[single_statement] { … }` pattern into a direct
  `let s = …; fconn.execute(s); rconn.execute_batch(s);` sequence.
  The for-loop was only ever iterating once and clippy flagged it
  as unnecessary. No behavioural change.

crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/benchmark.rs
- rustfmt: single
  `let sw_meta = BenchmarkComparisonMetadata::anonymous(…)` call
  that was split across two lines rewraps to a single line now
  that the identifiers fit.

crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/complete_benchmark_matrix.rs
- `sample_benchmark_summary` fixture: add
  `comparison: None, aggregated_hot_path: None` to the
  BenchmarkSummary literal so it matches the struct shape after
  the aggregated_hot_path field addition (5e4486d3). Previous
  layout put `comparison: None` after `iterations: …`; moved it
  above iterations to group all Option<T> fields together and to
  keep the literal order matching the struct definition.
- This was the only remaining caller missing the new field — with
  it, `cargo check -p fsqlite-e2e --all-targets` goes green.
